raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to formally express my dissatisfaction with Citi Bank 's handling of multiple disputes I initiated against XXXX for charges amounting to {$75.00} each. Despite providing all necessary documentation, including the letter I received from XXXX, which I also submitted to the XXXX XXXX XXXX and proof of refunds on my XXXX account, my Citi Bank account continues to reflect an outstanding balance. 

The specific details of my situation are as follows : First Charge ( {$75.00} ) : Date of Payment : XX/XX/year> Card Ending : XXXX Dispute Initiated : XX/XX/year> Dispute Closed in My Favor : XX/XX/year> According to XXXX letter, Citi Bank was responsible for crediting the funds back to my account once the dispute was resolved in my favor. However, my Citi Bank account still shows this amount as due. 
Second Charge ( {$75.00} ) : Date of Payment : XX/XX/year> Card Ending : XXXX Dispute Initiated : XX/XX/year> Dispute Closed in My Favor : XX/XX/year> XXXX confirms that the funds were pulled by Citi Bank for investigation, and the dispute was again resolved in my favor. Yet, my Citi Bank account does not reflect this refund either. 
Third Charge ( {$75.00} ) : Date of Payment : XX/XX/year> Card Ending : XXXX XXXX has successfully refunded this charge and stated that it may take 5-7 business days for the funds to appear. However, this refund is not reflected in my account, nor have the previous disputes been properly resolved. 
As it stands, I should have a XXXX balance on my account, and all disputes should be closed. The failure of Citi Bank to properly credit the refunds after multiple disputes were settled in my favor is causing undue stress and inconvenience. I request an immediate review and resolution of these issues, with all applicable funds credited to my account without further delay. 

Please confirm receipt of this complaint and provide a timeline for resolution. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
